mnemonics.title: PDA to CFG Conversion
mnemonics.content: Remember PDA to CFG uses 'Configuration Variables': [pXq] means from state p with X on stack, reach state q. The PDA starts at q₀ with S and ends at any state, represented as [q₀ S qf].

mnemonics.title: CNF Conversion Steps
mnemonics.content: Use mnemonic: 'EE PURR' - Eliminate ε, Eliminate unit, Put terminals, Use binaries, Remove useless. Order: ε → unit → terminals → binaries → useless symbols.

mnemonics.title: CFL Closure Properties
mnemonics.content: CFLs are closed under Union, Concat, Star, Homomorphism, and Intersection with Regular. NOT closed under Intersection or Complement. Remember: 'U C S H I R' for closed, 'I C' for not closed.

mnemonics.title: Pumping Lemma Decomposition
mnemonics.content: For CFL pumping lemma remember: w = uvxyz with |vy|≥1 (non-empty pumped part) and |vxy|≤p (pumped part bounded). Pumping increases/decreases middle section, not edges.

acronyms.usage: CFG where productions are A→a or A→BC, used for parsing algorithms like CYK
CFG where productions start with a terminal, useful for top-down parsing and proving PDA equivalence
Dynamic programming algorithm for CFL membership testing in O(n³)
Automaton with stack memory, recognizes CFLs
Grammar with single non-terminal on LHS, generates CFLs
Language generated by CFG, recognized by PDA
Set of states reachable via ε-transitions, used in PDA construction

visualTips.visualization: Imagine stack as a 'derivation recorder'. When you see a non-terminal A on stack top, you 'expand' it by choosing one of its productions - you pop A and push the production symbols. Terminals are simply matched and popped. The stack grows when you expand non-terminals, shrinks when you match terminals.

Think of parse tree as an upside-down tree. Root = start symbol S. Each branching = production application. Leaves = terminals form the string (left-to-right). Like a family tree showing how each symbol 'gave birth' to more symbols.

Visualize uvxyz as three parts: u v x y z. The 'pumped' middle portion (vy) is in the middle, can be repeated (doubled) or removed (made empty). The bounded part vxy stays within pumping length p. Think of it like: u [v y] x [v y] z where bracketed part can be pumped.

Think of CYK table as triangular matrix. Fill diagonal first with terminals. Then build upward: cell (i,j) depends on splitting at all positions k: check if A→BC where B in (i,k) and C in (k+1,j). Like building pyramid from bottom - each level depends on two cells below it.

analogies.analogy: A PDA is like a typewriter that can also use a stack of papers. It can write symbols (push), read from top (pop), and look without removing (top). It accepts only if the final stack state matches what was expected - like checking if parentheses are balanced.

A CFG is like a recipe book. Non-terminals are 'categories' (like 'dessert'), terminals are 'ingredients'. Productions are 'recipes' telling how to make one category from others. Following recipes gives you the final 'dish' (the string).

CFG and PDA are like English and French - both can express the same ideas (languages) but in different ways. The 'translation' between them preserves meaning (language). Just as you can write in either language, you can generate or recognize with either formalism.

Converting to CNF or GNF is like standardizing document formats. CNF is like requiring all documents to be either single sentences (A→a) or two-ingredient instructions (A→BC). GNF requires starting each instruction with an action word (terminal). Makes processing easier.
